description: University medical studies must stay scoped to medieval university and Salerno medical-school curriculum, not generic herbal, distillation, or early-anatomy practice.

checks.reason: The node is anchored to the mature eleventh- to twelfth-century Salerno and medieval university medical-curriculum tradition.
The scoped technology is formal medical study in a university or university-like medical-school setting.
Salerno formalized earlier learned medical traditions into medieval university curricula.
Scholastic textual methods are contextual university-study infrastructure, not the institutional setting itself.
Distillation is a pharmaceutical technique, not the direct prerequisite for the curriculum node.
Herbal preparation is too narrow for the learned-medical curriculum lineage.
Early anatomy should not be a direct prerequisite unless the node is narrowed to anatomy teaching.
